heroSummary: A daily directional snapshot of worldwide risk conditions across conflict, economy, public health, and climate. Today’s update keeps global risk firmly high and slightly higher than yesterday as Hormuz-linked conflict, oil-export disruption, and shipping insecurity continue to outweigh the partial relief coming from emergency stock releases and Fujairah’s restart.
overallExplainer: The composite score remains high because conflict risk is still transmitting through shipping, energy infrastructure, oil supply, insurance, freight, and market confidence channels. Health risk remains moderate rather than low, while climate risk continues to sit as a persistent structural background pressure.

overview.label: What Changed
overview.sub: Conflict stayed severe despite reserve action

glanceSummary: The index moved modestly higher because the core Gulf risk picture still has not normalized. Partial operational recovery at Fujairah and emergency stock releases help reduce immediate panic, but they do not remove the underlying conflict, supply, shipping, and confidence shock. Health conditions remain moderate because cholera and measles still prevent a low-risk baseline, while climate risk stays structurally elevated under warm seasonal guidance.
glanceBullets: Conflict risk remained the clearest global driver as Hormuz insecurity, coalition pressure, and retaliation risk continued to dominate the near-term outlook.
Economic risk stayed high because oil, insurance, and freight stress remain elevated even with IEA and national reserve releases.
Health risk stayed moderate with cholera burden and measles activity still visible across international monitoring.
Climate risk remained persistently high because seasonal guidance still favors widespread above-normal land temperatures.

riskData.title: 🌍 Global Conflict Risk
riskData.subtitle: Hormuz insecurity, export-hub stress, and retaliation pressure
riskData.description: Conflict risk remains the dominant global driver. The immediate threat environment still extends beyond direct strikes into tanker security, oil-export infrastructure, allied naval posture, and regional retaliation scenarios, keeping the geopolitical backdrop extremely unstable.

riskData.title: 💰 Global Economic Risk
riskData.subtitle: Oil shock, freight insurance, and inflation sensitivity
riskData.description: Economic risk remains high. Emergency reserve releases and Japan’s stock draw help soften the most acute supply panic, but they do not remove the broader macro pressure coming from disrupted flows, higher crude prices, shipping risk, and cost transmission into fuel and inflation.

riskData.title: 🏥 Global Health Risk
riskData.subtitle: Persistent cholera burden and continued measles pressure
riskData.description: Health risk remains moderate rather than low. There is still no single pandemic-scale shock dominating the dashboard, but ongoing cholera monitoring and sustained measles pressure continue to signal resilience gaps in routine surveillance, immunization, and outbreak control.

riskData.title: 🌡️ Climate Risk
riskData.subtitle: Warm-season stress and widespread temperature anomalies
riskData.description: Climate risk remains structurally elevated. Seasonal outlooks continue to indicate a widespread signal for above-normal land temperatures, keeping pressure high across agriculture, water systems, infrastructure, wildfire exposure, and humanitarian resilience.

reportData.title: 🌍 Global Conflict Report
reportData.watch: Hormuz transit, coalition buildup, export-site targeting
reportData.why: Conflict risk remains the top category because the current Gulf crisis still threatens both military and commercial arteries at the same time. The danger now spans direct confrontation, shipping access, export terminals, and allied response posture across one of the world’s most critical energy corridors.
reportData.moved: The score moved slightly higher because the underlying confrontation remains unresolved and still capable of producing fresh infrastructure or maritime shocks. Fujairah’s restart helps, but it does not yet change the wider risk regime.
reportData.matters: Conflict risk matters because it feeds directly into energy flows, trade costs, market psychology, humanitarian pressure, and wider regional instability. It remains the strongest reason the composite index stays elevated.

reportData.title: 💰 Global Economic Report
reportData.watch: Oil flows, reserve delivery, insurance pricing, freight costs
reportData.why: The global economy remains exposed to a war-driven energy and logistics shock. Emergency oil releases reduce some near-term panic, but the broader disruption risk remains tied to actual shipping insecurity and the possibility of renewed export losses.
reportData.moved: The score edged higher because relief measures are arriving into a still-unresolved conflict environment. That means price spikes may soften at the margin while the underlying vulnerability remains high.
reportData.matters: Economic risk matters because it is the main channel through which geopolitics reaches households and businesses: fuel prices, transport costs, inflation pressure, fiscal strain, and confidence.

reportData.title: 🏥 Global Health Report
reportData.watch: Cholera monitoring, measles cases, immunity gaps
reportData.why: Health conditions remain below the intensity of conflict and energy risk in today’s dashboard, but not low enough to fade from view. Cholera remains a multi-country concern and measles continues to reflect weaknesses in routine immunization and surveillance.
reportData.moved: The score stayed broadly unchanged because there is no new dominant pandemic-style emergency today. The pressure remains persistent rather than sharply accelerating.
reportData.matters: Health risk matters because it compounds humanitarian fragility and can intensify the downstream effects of conflict, displacement, climate stress, and weaker public services.

reportData.title: 🌡️ Climate Risk Report
reportData.watch: Heat anomalies, crop stress, wildfire and water pressure
reportData.why: Climate risk remains structurally high even on days dominated by war and markets. Seasonal guidance still points to widespread above-normal land temperatures, which keeps the climate layer of the dashboard elevated.
reportData.moved: The score stayed broadly unchanged because the climate backdrop remains severe but was not the sharpest same-day mover in the dashboard. This is a chronic structural pressure rather than a sudden headline-driven jump.
reportData.matters: Climate risk matters because it amplifies food stress, water pressure, infrastructure loss, insurance strain, migration pressure, and public-health vulnerability.
